openmeter/subscription/validators/custom.purpose: Implements customer.RequestValidator to enforce subscription-aware pre-conditions on customer mutations — blocks deletes when active subscriptions exist and prevents subject-key changes when a customer has active subscriptions. Registered at wiring time via app/common to avoid circular imports.

patterns.name: NoopRequestValidator embedding
patterns.description: Embed customer.NoopRequestValidator so only the overridden methods need implementation; unimplemented lifecycle methods default to no-op.
patterns.example: type Validator struct { customer.NoopRequestValidator; subscriptionService subscription.Service; customerService customer.Service }

patterns.name: Interface assertion at package level
patterns.description: Declare var _ customer.RequestValidator = (*Validator)(nil) at package top for compile-time interface satisfaction proof.

patterns.name: Constructor nil-guard
patterns.description: NewValidator returns (*Validator, error) and validates every dependency is non-nil before construction.
patterns.example: if subscriptionService == nil { return nil, fmt.Errorf("subscription service is required") }

patterns.name: Use clock.Now() for time-sensitive queries
patterns.description: Pass lo.ToPtr(clock.Now()) to subscription.ListSubscriptionsInput.ActiveAt so tests can control time via the clock package.

patterns.name: Delegate input validation to input type
patterns.description: Call input.Validate() first inside each handler before any domain queries to keep validation self-contained.
patterns.example: if err := input.Validate(); err != nil { return err }

key_file_guides.role: Single file implementing the customer.RequestValidator interface with ValidateUpdateCustomer and ValidateDeleteCustomer logic.
key_file_guides.watch_for: ValidateUpdateCustomer fetches the current customer only when UsageAttribution.SubjectKeys is non-nil — skipping this branch for other mutations is intentional. Error types must use models.NewGenericNotFoundError / models.NewGenericPreConditionFailedError for correct HTTP status mapping.

anti_patterns: Returning plain fmt.Errorf for not-found or pre-condition errors — must use models.NewGenericNotFoundError / models.NewGenericPreConditionFailedError so HTTP mapping works correctly
Using context.Background() instead of the caller-supplied ctx
Calling billing or other non-subscription services from this validator — would create import cycles
Checking active subscriptions without the ActiveAt filter — would return already-cancelled subscriptions
Registering the validator inside the subscription or customer package constructors — must be done in app/common to avoid circular imports

decisions.decision: Validator is registered via customer.Service.RegisterRequestValidator() in app/common wiring, not hardcoded in the customer package.
decisions.rationale: Avoids circular import between subscription and customer packages; subscription registers the constraint at startup through the registry pattern.

decisions.decision: Only ValidateUpdateCustomer and ValidateDeleteCustomer are overridden; ValidateCreateCustomer remains a no-op via embedding.
decisions.rationale: Customer creation has no subscription pre-conditions; embedding NoopRequestValidator avoids boilerplate.

code_examples.scenario: Register the validator during app wiring (app/common)
code_examples.code:
import customer "github.com/openmeterio/openmeter/openmeter/subscription/validators/customer"

v, err := customer.NewValidator(subscriptionService, customerService)
if err != nil { return err }
customerService.RegisterRequestValidator(v)

openmeter/subscription/validators/subscr.purpose: Implements subscription.SubscriptionCommandHook to enforce the unique-constraint invariant: no two active subscriptions for the same customer may overlap in time or cover the same features. Registered at wiring time via app/common; feature-flag-gated for multi-subscription support.

patterns.name: NoOpSubscriptionCommandHook embedding
patterns.description: Embed subscription.NoOpSubscriptionCommandHook so only the overridden Before*/After* methods need implementation.

patterns.name: Config struct with Validate()
patterns.description: Group all dependencies in a Config struct with its own Validate() method; the constructor calls config.Validate() and returns an error if any dependency is missing.
patterns.example: func (c SubscriptionUniqueConstraintValidatorConfig) Validate() error { if c.FeatureFlags == nil { return fmt.Errorf("feature flags is required") } ... }

patterns.name: Pipeline composition through small component methods
patterns.description: Split validation into named steps (collectCustomerSubscriptionsStarting, mapSubsToViews, mapViewsToSpecs, validateUniqueConstraint, includeSubSpec) in components.go and compose them in validator.go; each step returns (result, error).
patterns.example: subs, _ := v.collectCustomerSubscriptionsStarting(...); views, _ := v.mapSubsToViews(ctx, subs); specs, _ := v.mapViewsToSpecs(views); _, err = v.validateUniqueConstraint(ctx, specs)

patterns.name: Feature-flag-gated validation path
patterns.description: Check ffx.Service.IsFeatureEnabled for subscription.MultiSubscriptionEnabledFF before choosing which validation path to run; BeforeUpdate skips all validation when multi-subscription is disabled.

patterns.name: pagination.CollectAll for unbounded list queries
patterns.description: Use pagination.CollectAll + pagination.NewPaginator when fetching all customer subscriptions to avoid silently missing subscriptions beyond the first page.
patterns.example: return pagination.CollectAll(ctx, pagination.NewPaginator(func(ctx context.Context, page pagination.Page) (pagination.Result[subscription.Subscription], error) { return v.Config.QueryService.List(ctx, ...) }), 1000)

patterns.name: Exclude current subscription before re-inserting updated spec
patterns.description: In BeforeUpdate, filter out the subscription being updated (by ID) from the existing set before appending the target spec so the old version does not conflict with the new one.
patterns.example: views, err = v.filterSubViews(func(v subscription.SubscriptionView) bool { return v.Subscription.ID != currentId.ID }, views)

patterns.name: pipelineAfter reuse across post-mutation hooks
patterns.description: AfterCreate, AfterUpdate, AfterCancel, AfterContinue, and BeforeDelete all delegate to the single pipelineAfter method; use includeSubViewUnique (lo.UniqBy) to prevent double-counting.
patterns.example: func (v SubscriptionUniqueConstraintValidator) AfterCreate(ctx context.Context, view subscription.SubscriptionView) error { return v.pipelineAfter(ctx, view) }

key_file_guides.role: Implements all Before*/After* hook methods; each method builds a collect→map→validate→include pipeline.
key_file_guides.watch_for: pipelineAfter is reused for AfterCreate, AfterUpdate, AfterCancel, AfterContinue, and BeforeDelete — any change to post-mutation validation affects all five hooks simultaneously.

key_file_guides.role: Low-level pipeline building blocks (collect, map, filter, include) used by validator.go; no business rules live here.
key_file_guides.watch_for: includeSubViewUnique uses lo.UniqBy by subscription ID to prevent double-counting the same subscription; removing that dedup causes false-positive uniqueness errors.

anti_patterns: Querying subscriptions without the ActiveInPeriod filter — returns all-time subscriptions and breaks the uniqueness check
Calling subscription.Service write methods from within a hook — creates re-entrant hook calls
Using context.Background() instead of the caller-supplied ctx
Bypassing pagination.CollectAll for large customer subscription lists — silently misses subscriptions beyond the first page
Adding business logic directly into components.go — it is a pure utility file; keep domain decisions in validator.go

decisions.decision: Components are separated into components.go to keep validator.go focused on the validation pipeline logic.
decisions.rationale: Improves readability and testability of each pipeline step in isolation.

decisions.decision: Feature flag subscription.MultiSubscriptionEnabledFF selects between per-subscription and per-feature uniqueness checks.
decisions.rationale: Allows gradual rollout of multi-subscription support without breaking existing single-subscription customers.

decisions.decision: Hook is registered via subscription.Service.RegisterHook() at wiring time in app/common, not inside the subscription package itself.
decisions.rationale: Keeps the uniqueness constraint out of the core subscription package to avoid tight coupling and import cycles.

code_examples.scenario: Register the validator during app wiring (app/common)
